# Transcrição: 04-Lead Gen Example

## [00:00 - 03:03]

Hey, this is Deepash and in this example I'm going to show you how to set up your funnel using a lead generation example. So previously you probably have seen the full funnel system set up, an explanation of the full funnel system and an e-commerce example and in this one I'm going to show you two accounts where I use this for lead generation. So both accounts are digital products, both are actually course and coaching related. So I just want to demonstrate how I use the same accounts set up for digital products. So this account structure here is exactly as I would go through for any other kind of account. I have my cold audiences, I have my warm, hot and also existing customers. The objective is at the end of the naming convention and then I've labeled them 1, 2, 3 and 4 as per the full funnel system. So you can see this example, I've also got the dummy ads campaign where I will store my ads in a website conversion objective campaign for ads that I want to share the post idea of into my other campaign so I can share the social proof and things like that. So this dummy campaign is where I would go in and create my core ads. I would then use the post ID and then share that post within the different campaigns. So here the SMC1 cold PPE campaign, that's where I'm doing all of my graduation testing. So to give you an example, if I just click into here for this particular account, we've been testing a few lookalikes, some interests, etc. And you can see down the budgets column, they're all set at $5 a day. And so I'm testing multiple audiences at once with lots of different creatives to find the ones that are working and are resonating. Now with this one, we're running video and so we were trying to see if we can get engagement on those videos as well. And so if you can scroll across, we've got our video engagement metrics and trying to see which ones are working. So just in a nutshell, that's the graduation framework in play there. Then we have a warm audience where we are pushing ads into fans into email lists, etc. There's a video views campaign where I'm also doing some testing on the graduation alongside my page post engagement. Then my website conversion campaign, where I'm pushing my core audiences and my creatives and then testing with different optimized events. So trying to push people to the landing page view, also running some impression based campaigns just to see what kind of traction I can get. Now this particular account, it's a fairly mid to high ticket coaching program.

## [03:04 - 06:09]

So we're talking products that's in the thousands. And so therefore the customer journey for this particular account is to take people through a webinar. So my goal here is to generate email signups for the webinar. So the testing I'm doing for these audiences in PPVVVV, to push people from cold into warm into the webinar. If they don't sign up, then we're recapturing them in our hot audience through website conversion and running an impressions campaign with this specific account because the audience sizes are so small. So you can see here it's a 30 day campaign. And in the 30 days, there's only been just under 9,000 impressions on this particular asset. But that's where the bulk of the revenue has been made for this particular account. And then we've done some work with existing customers as well. But essentially, the funnel is cold ads into a webinar registration, watch the webinar, and then we'll retarget you via email and also via Facebook ads. If you don't watch the webinar, but you have registered them, we're also trying to recapture you back into the funnel to get you to watch that webinar as well. And ultimately, you can see this campaign, I'm just showing 30 days of stats. We've actually made, let's have a look. So we've spent $650. Now this has been spread between generating emails, and then also getting those, so getting those registrations and then converting. And you can see where the budget's been allocated to the majority of budget has gone to existing customers for this new product and also to our cold retargeting audience as well. And what that's generated for us on the back end is overall 2,900% return on investment. So you can see from the $650 that went in, we've generated just shy of $19,000. And that's purely through our existing email list and our cold prospecting. You can see actually our cold prospecting has brought in 5,800% return on ad spend, whereas our existing customers not so good. But overall, it's been a pretty good campaign. The next stage is to continue pumping our budget into our cold campaigns, continually keep churning people into that webinar, push them through the email sequences and get them to convert. And really, like I keep saying when it comes to digital products, the retargeting is where you're going to make your money. So that's just one example of a fairly mid to high ticket item. The second example I'll share here, this is a product where I think it's selling for something like $600 or $700. So it's not a massively high ticket. It's kind of like I say the low to mid level ticket item. And it's the same structure

## [06:09 - 08:57]

that we've got in place here as well. So again, we're looking at the last 30 days. And you can see we've split the campaigns out as per the full funnel system. We're only using three layers here because there's no targeting on existing customers. Once they're in, then they're into the course. So here we split out by objective. And you can see if I look at the performance for this particular one. So in this in this period, so this is 30 days, we spent 4k on this particular account. We've made 36k back. So that's a 40 pound cost per acquisition. And a 900% return on ad spend. So it's just to give you an idea of a slightly different product and a different price point. The process with this particular product because of the price point is we work on lead generation to capture people into an email sequence. And we give a PDF away, which is a guide and also a glimpse into what the what the course offers. And then we push them through an email sequence. And the majority of the sales actually come through email, and we shore up the rest of it in Facebook. So this particular launch, it was a limited time period. The total value of the launch was half a million pounds. And you can see 36,000 pounds of that actually came through Facebook. But if you actually put the numbers together, the 4000 pounds we put in has generated half a million in return. And you know, this isn't me saying that, you know, I'm fantastically great at Facebook advertising. What I want you to take away from this video is the for funnel approach, the graduation framework, all of the testing that goes in, it works on e-commerce, it works on lead journey or work on different sectors as well, it works on mobile app install on local campaigns, etc. And the key thing is to understand how to push people through different parts of the funnel to do the graduation framework testing, get your audiences and your crates is in play. If you're doing a digital product, you're probably going to push them into a webinar or some kind of download. It's all about high value and understanding what things are going to motivate them, what's going to appeal to them, and then being able to convert as many of those through email as possible, and then assuring up the rest through retargeting. So here, for example, you can see the retargeting campaign has brought in the majority of the revenue at about 3,100% ROAS. And so you can kind of see that retargeting has done its job here as well. And so that's kind of just to give you an idea of how to use that for your digital products and to make the full funnel system work for you.
